(31)? 10 ?- 2002 &RT ifi?hf?a fcl;,:rrlf<lT t1 MINISfRYOF FINANCE ( Department or Economic Affairs) NOTIF1CATION New Delhi, the 25th July, 2003 G.S.R 586(E).- Jn exercise of the powers conferred by section 15 of the Government Savings Banks Act, 1873 (5 of 1873), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Post Office Savings Bank General Rules, 1981, namely: - 1. (1) These rules may be called the Post Office Savings Bank General (Amendment) Rules, 2003. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in Official Gazette. 2. In the Post Office Savings Bank General Rules, 1981, • (i) rule 3 shall be re-numbered as sub-rule (1); (u) after sub-rule (1) as so re-numbered, the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:· "(2) Non-Resident Indians (NR!s) are not eligible to open an account in a Post Office Savings Bank: Provided that if a resident who opened an account in any Post Office Savings Bank, subsequently becomes Non-Resident Indian during the currency of maturity period, may continue such account till its maturity on a Non-Repatriation Basis". IF No. 7/1/2000 (NRI)-NS-1!] P C. SINGH. I NOTIFlCATION New Delhi, the 251h July. 2003 G.S.R587(E).- In exercise of the powers conferred by section 15 of the Government Savings Banks Act, 1873 (5 of 1873), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Post Office Savings Account Rules, 1981, namely: 1. (1) These rules may be called the Post Office Savings Account (Amendment) Rules, 2003. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Post Office Savings Account Rules, 1981, in rule 6, for sub-rule (2), the following sub-rule shall be substituted, namely:- -- 3 of 13 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA• EXTRAORDINARY [PARTII-SEC. 3(i)j "(2) Interest shall be allowed only in sums of complete rupees and shall be rounded off to nearest rupee and for this purpose any amount of 50 paise or more shall be treated as one rupee and any amount less than 50 paise shall be ignored.". IF.No. 217/2000-NS-ll (Vol.-!)] PC. SlNGH. Under Secy. (1) These rules may be called the Post Office Recurring Deposit (Second Amendment) Rules, 2003. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Post Office Recurring Deposit Rules, 1981: After rule 14, the following rule shall be inserted, namely:- "14 A. Mode of Payments:- All transactions of recoveries, with?rawals, repayments, etc. under these rules shall be rounded off to the nearest rupee and for this purpose any amount of 50 paise or more shall be treated as one rupee and any amount less than 50 paise shall be ignored.". [E No. 2/7/2000-NS-II(\.bl.-l)] P. C. SINGH, Under Secy. Fi;",i,r; 24 olJ@ 1992, m l!iT.f.r. 586 (:J!),? 2 ffi<'fl<R, 1993 m q;r f.r 118 (:J!), R8Jq;- 8 l!l'd, 1995, <'IT<lilr'! 3 (:l!), ? 1 ;Jf•fif?t 1999, <ll.Q>T.f.r 43 (:J!).f<;'lfQ;15 ':if'l<Rr,;>ooo. m.Q>T.f.r 151 (:J!),R'1t<li"1 m,t 2001 ffl.qij.f.r_ 159 (:J!),mq;- 1 'IT{ 2002. mq;rf.r 174 (:J!)R'lii'li 1 rinl 2003, &Rf mitfun fii,"11)Tflll ?I • -- 6 of 13 -- 7 NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 25th July, 2003 G.S.R. 589(E).-· In exercise of the powers conferred by section 15 of the uovernment Savings Banks Act, 1873 (5 of 1873), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules to further to amend the Post Office Time Deposit Rules, 1981, namely:- 1. (1) These rules may be called the Post Office Time Deposit (Second Amendment) Rules, 4003. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Post Office Time Deposit Rules, 1981,· (a) in rule 5, in sub-rule (1), for the words "fifty rupees" the words "two hundrea rupees" shall be substituted; (b) rule 7 shall be re-numbered as rule 7(1) thereof and after rule 7(1) as so re- numbered the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely :- (2) "Where the interest contains part of a rupee and if such part is fifty paise or more, it shall be rounded off to one complete rupee, and if such part is less than fifty parse, it shall be ignored". · IF No. 2/U/2002-NS-UJ PC. SINGH. Under Secy. Note : The Principal rules were published in the Gazette of India vide number GSR 664(E), dated 1)'11,December, 1981 and subsequently amended vide GSR 300(E), dated 1'' April, 1982, GSR 257(E), dated the 1111'March, 1983, GSR 502(E) dated the 9•hJuly, 1984 GSR 418(E), dated the lO'h May, 1985, GSR 193(E), dated the 121hFebruary, 1986, GSR 362(E), dated the 1'1 April 1987, GSR 1005(E), dated the 23'' December, 1987, GSR 353 (E) dated 18'" March, 1988, GSR 507(E), dated the 23'' May, 1990, GSR 191(E), dated the 27'h March, 1991, GSR 580(E), dated the 12'" September, 1991, GSR 727 (E), dated the 6'h December, 1991, GSR 431 (E) dated the 24'h April, 1992, GSR 586 (E), dated the 2"' September, 1993, GSR 118 (E) dated 8'" March 1995, GSR 3(E) dated 1'' January, 1999, GSR 43(E) dated the 15'" January, 2000, GSR 151(E), dated the 1" March, 2001, GSR 159(E) 1" March, 2002 and GSR 174(E) dated 1" March, 2003. -- 7 of 13 -- THE GAZE1TE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY ? ,rl fu.m, 25 ?. 2003 [PARTII-SEC. Tf(( '11I • • • -- 8 of 13 -- [ 'lT'T n-= 3(i) l 9 NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 25th July. 2003 G.S.R.590(E).-In exercise of the powers conferred by section 12 of the Government Savings Certificates Act, 1959 (46 of 1959), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the National Savings Certificates (VIII Issue) Rules, 1989, namely:- 1. (1) These rules may be called the National Savings Certificates (VIII Issue) (Second Amendment) Rules, 2003. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the National Savings Certificates (VIII Issue), 1989, (i) in rule 15, after sub-rule (5), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:- " (6) The amount payable at the time of encashment on maturity may be rounded off to the nearest multiple of one rupee and for this purpose any amount of 50 paise or more shall be treated as one rupee and any amountless than 50 paise shall be ignored.". (ii) in rule 16, after sub-rule (4), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:- " (5) The amount payable at the time of premature encashment may be rounded off to the nearest multiple of one rupee and for this purpose any amount of 50 paise or more shall be treated as one rupee and any amount less than 50 paise shall be ignored.". [F.No. 2/7/2000-NS-Il(\bl.-l)) P. C. SINGH, Under Secy. - Note : The principal rules were published vide GSR 496 (E) dated 1st May, 1989 and subsequently amended vide GSR 508 (E) dated 23'd May, 1990, GSR 120 (E) dated 8th March, 1998, GSR 7(E) dated 151January, 1999, GSR 491 (E) dated 5th July, 1999, GSR 47(E) dated 15th January, 2000, GSR 156(E) dated 1st March, 2001, GSR 572(E) dated 2nd August, 2001, GSR 163(E) dated 1st March, 2002, GSR 711(E) dated 17thOctober, 2002 and GSR 179(E) dated 1st March, 2003. -- 9 of 13 -- IO THE GAZE'JTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY ? ? fu.m, 25 ?. 2003 IPARTn?SEc. 3(i)] 1 m:<lir.f.l. I I -- 10 of 13 -- 11 NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 2.5th July, 2003 G.&R.S9I(E).- In exercise of the powers conferred by section 12 of the Government Savings Certificates Act, 1959 (46 of 1959), the Central Government . hereby makes the folloWing rules further to amend the National Savings Certificates (VIII Issue) Rules, 1989, namely: - 1. (1) These rules may be called the National Savings Certificates (VIII Issue) (Third Amendment) Rules, 2003. (2) They sha II come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the National Savings Certificates (VIII Issue), 1989, in rule 4, after sub-rule (2), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely: - "(3) Non-Resident Indians (NRis) are not eligible to purchase the National Savings Certificates: Provided that if a resident who subsequently becomes NRI during the currency of maturity period, shall be allowed to avail the benefits of the certificates on maturity on a non-repatriation basis." [F.No. 7/1/2000 (NRI)-NS-II] P. C. SINGH. Under Secy. Note : The principal rules were published vide GSR 496 (E) dated the 1st May, 1989 and subsequently amended vide GSR 508 (E) dated the 23'd May, 1990, GSR 120 (E) dated the 8th March, 1998, GSR 7(E) dated the i" January, 1999, GSR 491 (E) dated the 6th July, 1999, GSR 47(E) dated the is" January, 2000, GSR 156(E) dated the 1st March, 2001, GSR 572(E) dated the 2nctAugust, 2001, GSR 163(E) dated the i" March, 2002, GSR 7ll(E) dated the 1 Jlh October, 2002 and GSR 179(E) dated the 1st March, 2003. , -- 11 of 13 -- 12====================1BE??G?AZE??ll?E?O?f?IND?IA?·?.EXTRA??O?RD?1N?AR? 3lf? ? f<;tti\, 25 ?' 2003 m.oi;r.f.t 592(31).- ? ' -- 12 of 13 -- ['ll'TII-"«lll!3(i)] 13 NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 2.5thJuly,2003 G.s.R.592(E).- In exercise of the powers conferred by section 12 of the Government Savings Certificates Act, 1959 (46 of 1959), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Kisan Vikas Patra Rules, 1988, namely:- 1. (1) These rules may be called the Kisan Vikas Patra (Second Amendment) Rules, 2003. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Kisan Vikas Patra Rules, 1988, in rule 6, after sub-rule (2), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:- "(3) Non-Resident Indians (NRis) are not eligible to purchase the Kisan Vikas Patras: Provided that if a resident who subsequently becomes NRI during the currency of maturity period, shall be allowed to avail the benefits of the Patras on maturity on a non-repatriation basis." [F.No. 7 /In.OCYJ (NR])-NS-II] P. C. SINGH, Under Secy. Note : The principal rules were published vide GSR 370 (E) dated the 22nd March, 1988 and subsequently amended vide GSR 81 (E) dated the 5th February, 1989, GSR 8 (E) dated the 4th January, 1990, GSR 728(E) dated the 5th December, 1991, GSR 432 (E) dated the 24th April, 1992, GSR 567(E) dated the 20thAugust, 1993, GSR 588(E) dated the z= September, 1993, GSR 119(E) dated the 8th March, 1995, GSR 4(E) dated the 1st January, 1999, GSR 492(E) dated the 6th July, 1999, GSR 659(E) dated the 23rd September, 1999, GSR 660(E), dated the 23rd September,1999, GSR 765(E), dated the 11th November, 1999, GSR 48(E), dated the 15th January, 2000, GSR 155(E), dated the 1st March, 2001, GSR 164(E), dated the 1st March, 2002 and GSR 180(E), dated the i" March, 2003.
